title: Getting Started with PT Performance
content:
# Welcome to PT Performance

PT Performance is your comprehensive platform for managing rehabilitation programs and tracking your recovery progress.

## Key Features

### For Patients
- **Daily Readiness Check**: Log how you're feeling each day to help optimize your training
- **Exercise Programs**: Access your personalized exercise programs with video demonstrations
- **Session Scheduling**: View and manage your upcoming therapy sessions
- **Progress Tracking**: Monitor your strength gains and adherence over time

### For Therapists
- **Patient Management**: View and manage all your patients in one place
- **Program Builder**: Create custom exercise programs tailored to each patient
- **Readiness Monitoring**: Track patient readiness and adjust programs accordingly
- **Analytics**: Review patient progress with comprehensive analytics

## Getting Started

1. **Log In**: Use your email and password to access your account
2. **Complete Your Profile**: Add any relevant health information
3. **Review Your Program**: Check out your assigned exercises and upcoming sessions
4. **Start Training**: Follow your program and log your sessions

## Need Help?

Browse our help articles by category or use the search function to find specific topics.

title: How to Use Daily Readiness Check-In
content:
# Daily Readiness Check-In

The Daily Readiness Check-In helps you and your therapist understand how you're feeling and adjust your training accordingly.

## What is Readiness?

Readiness is a measure of how prepared your body is for training. It considers:
- **Pain Levels**: Current pain or discomfort
- **Sleep Quality**: How well you slept
- **Energy Levels**: Overall fatigue or energy
- **Stress**: Mental and physical stress

## How to Complete Your Check-In

1. **Access the Check-In**: Tap the "Today" tab and look for "Daily Check-In"
2. **Rate Your Status**: Use the sliders to rate different aspects of your readiness
3. **Add Notes**: Include any specific concerns or observations
4. **Submit**: Your therapist will review and may adjust your program

## Readiness Bands

- **Green (High)**: You're ready for your full program
- **Yellow (Moderate)**: Some adjustments may be needed
- **Red (Low)**: Significant modifications recommended

## When to Check In

Complete your check-in:
- Every morning before training
- Before each scheduled session
- Whenever you experience significant changes in how you feel

## Tips for Accuracy

- Be honest about how you feel
- Don't push through severe pain
- Note any changes in symptoms
- Communicate with your therapist about concerns

title: Understanding Your Exercise Program
content:
# Understanding Your Exercise Program

Your exercise program is custom-designed by your therapist to help you reach your recovery goals.

## Program Structure

### Sessions
Your program is organized into sessions, typically 2-4 per week. Each session contains:
- Warm-up exercises
- Main strength/mobility work
- Cool-down activities

### Exercise Details
Each exercise includes:
- **Sets & Reps**: How many times to perform the exercise
- **Load**: Weight or resistance to use
- **Rest Period**: How long to rest between sets
- **Video Demo**: Proper technique demonstration
- **Notes**: Special instructions from your therapist

## Following Your Program

### Before Your Session
1. Complete your daily readiness check-in
2. Review the exercises in your session
3. Watch video demonstrations for new exercises
4. Gather any equipment you'll need

### During Your Session
1. Start with the warm-up
2. Follow the prescribed sets, reps, and loads
3. Take appropriate rest periods
4. Log each exercise as you complete it
5. Note any difficulties or pain

### After Your Session
1. Complete the session summary
2. Rate your overall effort and pain
3. Add any notes for your therapist

## Program Adjustments

Your therapist may adjust your program based on:
- Your readiness scores
- Progress and performance
- Feedback and symptoms
- Phase advancement

## Exercise Modifications

If an exercise causes pain or difficulty:
1. Stop the exercise immediately
2. Log the issue in your session notes
3. Contact your therapist
4. Don't substitute exercises without approval

## Progression

As you improve, your program will advance through:
- Increased load/resistance
- More challenging exercises
- Higher volume (sets/reps)
- Reduced rest periods

title: How to Log Your Exercise Sessions
content:
# Logging Your Exercise Sessions

Accurate logging helps your therapist track your progress and make informed decisions about your program.

## Starting a Session

1. **Navigate to Today**: Tap the "Today" tab
2. **Select Your Session**: Tap on the scheduled session
3. **Complete Check-In**: If you haven't already, complete your readiness check-in
4. **Begin**: Tap "Start Session" when you're ready

## Logging Exercises

### For Each Exercise

1. **Review the Exercise**: Check sets, reps, and load prescribed
2. **Watch the Video**: Review proper technique if needed
3. **Perform the Exercise**: Complete the prescribed work
4. **Log Your Sets**: Tap each set to log:
   - Reps completed
   - Load used
   - RPE (Rate of Perceived Exertion)
   - Any modifications made

### Quick Logging

- Swipe right to mark a set as completed with prescribed values
- Swipe left to skip a set and note why
- Tap "Same as Prescribed" to quickly log matching values

## Session Notes

Add notes about:
- How exercises felt
- Any pain or discomfort experienced
- Equipment limitations
- Questions for your therapist

## Completing Your Session

1. **Finish All Exercises**: Work through your entire program
2. **Session Summary**: Review what you completed
3. **Overall Rating**: Rate your session:
   - Pain level (0-10)
   - Difficulty (1-10)
   - Completion (% of prescribed work)
4. **Submit**: Save your session data

## What If I Can't Complete Everything?

That's okay! Log what you did complete and note why you stopped:
- Time constraints
- Fatigue
- Pain or discomfort
- Equipment issues

## Tips for Accurate Logging

- Log in real-time during your session
- Be honest about loads and reps
- Note any modifications you made
- Report pain immediately
- Save your session when complete

## Reviewing Past Sessions

Access your history to:
- Review previous performance
- Track load progression
- Identify patterns
- Share with your therapist
